title: × Halimiocistus atlanticus
taxonomy: × Halimiocistus atlanticus is a hybrid species, resulting from the cross between Cistus ladanifer and Halimium umbellatum. This classification places it within the family Cistaceae. As a member of the Cistaceae family, × Halimiocistus atlanticus shares characteristics with its parent species. Its classification reflects its unique genetic makeup and physical appearance. The combination of its parent species' traits makes it a distinct and interesting member of the Cistaceae family.
properties: × Halimiocistus atlanticus, also known as Halimiocistus wirtgenstianus, is a species of flowering plant. Unfortunately, there is limited information available on the therapeutic properties of this specific plant species. However, some sources suggest that plants in the Cistus genus, to which × Halimiocistus atlanticus belongs, have been used in traditional medicine for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties may be beneficial in treating wounds, skin irritations, and other minor health issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential therapeutic benefits of × Halimiocistus atlanticus.

preparations: × Halimiocistus atlanticus is a shrub used in traditional herbal medicine, primarily in the Mediterranean region.
